title: Ouratea sagotii
intro: Ouratea sagotii, also known as the "Sagot's Ouratea", is a plant species in the family Ochnaceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The health benefits of Ouratea sagotii include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as skin infections and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Ouratea sagotii include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, often used to treat skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory problems.
taxonomy: Ouratea sagotii is classified within the family Ochnaceae. It belongs to the order Malpighiales. The plant is also known to be a part of the clade rosids. Further classification places it in the subclass Rosidae, and in the clade superrosids. It is also classified as a dicot, within the group Angiosperms.
